title: Columbia Student RANKS the Ivy Leagues...
body: To the high schoolers and young people that I imagine watch these videos types of videos, please do not put too much weight on a university’s ranking when making your decision. It’s nearly impossible to objectively rank a university and most of, if not all of the ranking methods are flawed in some way. In academia these rankings are NEVER considered and some of the best research is coming out of schools that a lot of students will write off due to their ranking.
author: @michaels8902

title: What do Christians have against homosexuality? | Tim Keller at Columbia University
body: Jesus’ message has always been “go and sin no more.” This message has been given to repentant sinners. He has never legitimized any sin.

title: What do Christians have against homosexuality? | Tim Keller at Columbia University
body: This guy kept skirting the question. His main point about making Christ Lord of your life was correct...so he had some truth in there. But what he didn't say is sin is sin. And we are all guilty of sin. The only way to heaven is thru Jesus....that part he got right. And when I was gay and God changed my life the people in church were loving and accepting without saying my sin was not sin. I thank God for all the love I received thru Christians.
author: @MrCharlesEldredge

title: What do Christians have against homosexuality? | Tim Keller at Columbia University
body: You can love your neighbor and not agree to go along with sin and call it normal. It is about the truth it is about repentance.
author: @nanettegray9674

title: What do Christians have against homosexuality? | Tim Keller at Columbia University
body:
A church i used to attend excommunicated a man for committing adultery. And it wasn't just because he committed the sin, it was because he refused to stop committing it. Jesus said, "Go and sin no more." While this is a tall order for us pathetic humans mired in sin, nonetheless, you have to come to terms with the fact that you have to admit that what you are doing is wrong, repent and try and sin no more.
A lot of folks are skipping over the "sin no more," part and just staying comfortable in their sin. No. Since we are all drowning and die in our sin, and Jesus is the only one who can pull us out of the water, we can't just sit back in the water and refuse His hand.
author: @anthonyharmon50

title: What do Christians have against homosexuality? | Tim Keller at Columbia University
body:
PSALMS 1
1Blessed is the man that walketh not in the counsel of the ungodly, nor standeth in the way of sinners, nor sitteth in the seat of the scornful.
2But his delight is in the law of the Lord; and in his law doth he meditate day and night.
3And he shall be like a tree planted by the rivers of water, that bringeth forth his fruit in his season; his leaf also shall not wither; and whatsoever he doeth shall prosper.
4The ungodly are not so: but are like the chaff which the wind driveth away.
5Therefore the ungodly shall not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous.
6For the Lord knoweth the way of the righteous: but the way of the ungodly shall perish.
author: @biblicalchristianity4918

title: What do Christians have against homosexuality? | Tim Keller at Columbia University
body: The interviewer should have asked Keller “Is there any need for the greedy to turn from their sin?” After Keller answers in the affirmative, follow up with “Is that also true for practicing homosexuality?”
author: @jg4x

title: What do Christians have against homosexuality? | Tim Keller at Columbia University
body: When I read and study the Bible with excellent teachers (mainly Calvary Chapel), I see that we are supposed to deny the flesh and try to follow well (with the help of Holy Spirit). Holy Spirit makes me notice or realize my pride or bitterness or bad attitude, my laziness and gluttony (whatever it is that needs to change). For instance, gossip is a form of murder, which I didn’t realize growing up as a “Christian” by tradition. I’m not supposed to just continue doing what I like to do (that is not how God wants me to be) but am supposed to ask God to keep convicting me and changing me to be more like Jesus. My son is about to live with his fiancé. This is wrong according to the Bible, and I pray that God’s will be done. I’ve discussed it and have asked my son to pray about it. Only God knows what He will and will not accept, and my concern is that I want to stay on the narrow road and I want to care about God’s laws. I do love gay people & because I love them, I would say that the Bible doesn’t condone acting out that lifestyle. If we didn’t love them, we wouldn’t care. Jesus said in Matthew 7:13 “Enter through the narrow gate.For wide is the gate and broad is the road that leads to destruction, and many enter through it. 14 But small is the gate and narrow the road that leads to life, and only a few find it”. I don’t mean any harm to gay people, but this is something serious for them to consider and take to The Lord in prayer.  🙏🏼🙏🏼🙏🏼🙏🏼 for everyone in this world, always!
author: @jaycole2109

title: What do Christians have against homosexuality? | Tim Keller at Columbia University
body: Love the sinner, Hate the sin! Call out the sin in such a way it can be received. It is Gods grace that brings you to repentance. Love covers a multitude of sin. Love them!!!!!!!!!

title: If Cornell was honest #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague #cornell
body: I went to Cornell and made a ton of important connections and when I applied to grad school (medical school) a ton of my interviewers were Cornell alum and we were able to connect and just hit off immediately due to the connection.  I eventually went to NYU, where a solid 10% of my classmates were Cornell alum and had a fantastic time.  I’m now starting my academic career in medicine on the west coast, where, again I run into and hit it off with a gaggle of former Cornell grads and have instant connection.  Cornell changed my life, when I can, I will donate back to my Alma mater

title: If Cornell was honest #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague #cornell
body: I attend Cornell, and there are countless aspects of my school that I genuinely value, including numerous activities like sports, games, club events and so on. I never feel "stuck" inside the walls of Cornell - In fact, I only wish I could have so much time to explore everything that my school has to offer. I never experience any form of inferiority complex because of my association with this institution. You mentioned Andy Bernard and I must confess that I didn't know who he was until you brought him up, so any reference to him is quite irrelevant. It's essential to recognize that allowing one individual to shape your perception of an entire group is a flawed approach. Not everyone gets admitted into a competitive school because of a good SAT score. Instances of legacy admissions are present in various prestigious institutions, including Harvard and Yale, and drawing broad conclusions based on such factors is, in essence, an immature perspective. Let's focus on appreciating the unique qualities of each academic institution without letting isolated incidents overshadow the collective experiences of its diverse student body.
author: @moreblessingmushohwe6506

title: Asking Columbia students about their major and expected salary💰#students #columbia #streetinterview
body: The two women who said salaries for Art history and human rights majors are $40k to $50k are practical.  But $85k for political science is on the high end (depending on career field post graduation).  I’m surprised students don’t do market research on salaries of industries they expect to go into upon graduation. Upside is Columbia University is located in NY city, so there should be many companies recruiting on campus and it should be easier to look for a job in the city.
author: @Orchid88-r6c

title: Steve Jobs' 2005 Stanford Commencement Address
body: “Your time is limited, so don’t waste it living someone else’s life. Don’t be trapped by dogma which is living with the results of other people’s thinking. Don’t let the noise of others’ opinions drown out your own inner voice. And the most important, is to have the courage to follow your heart and intuition. They somehow already know what you truly want to become. Everything else is secondary.”
author: @feliciaangkl

title: Steve Jobs' 2005 Stanford Commencement Address
body: I regularly comes here every month or whenever I got confused about life, facing chaos, less motivated and tired of hustling, this speech gives me hope & reason to fight. This is not just a speech but a philosophy of life. Thank you for this steve.
author: @chetanpanwar5703

title: Steve Jobs' 2005 Stanford Commencement Address
body:
Today is my 33rd birthday.
Somehow through the years this speech came back in my mind. Again and again. I watched it quite often now - almost once a year...
Years passed by and I always tried to stay hungry. Tried to stay foolish.
Today I am a well payed engineer in Germany. Working for the management of a huge global company. I became a private pilot. I do marathons and ultras...
...and I stay hungry. Looking forward for new achievements. And I stay foolish. I just applied for a management job for our hospitals. Trying to go beyond...
This speech is truely inspiring. Thank you, Steve! Thank you..
author: @mikeg.614

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body: Thank you all for the views and kind comments. We don't have anything to promote but would love a THANK YOU note/photo/video to share with our caregivers to give them strength as they battle COVID-19 from the frontlines. Please reply here or mention us on Twitter or Instagram (@AdventistHC) with your message of thanks to our nurses, doctors and team. #HeroesWorkHere
author: @adventisthealthcare

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body:
Being a cardiac surgeon is very tough,
I went through 2 years of vigorous training to become a cardiovascular surgeon but later i changed my speciality since i was not feeling very satisfied with these extreme long hours of operation and procesures.
Hats off to this brilliant soul, may god bless him.
author: @doctordoctor3266

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body: When he said "the important thing is I'm not nervous" I instantly felt much calmer and I don't even have to undergo heart surgery
author: @danielernst419

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body: As someone who has had 4 heart surgerys in my short lifetime (born with a heart defect) i can't thank this profession enough
author: @davidshoemaker9817

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body: As a paramedic, what he says at 2:35 is so true. Its really only "scary" when you have tried what you know should work/ MUST work, but it dosnt. An intubation for example, for your first time is definitely nerve wracking for obvious reasons, but after the first time its not scary until you nailed the whole process only to find its still not working, then you really have to think haha.
author: @Project_Future

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body: Fun fact: this man saved my fathers life. I will never forget the hours, minutes, seconds I sat waiting with my mom outside of surgery doors. For him to calmly come out, put his hand on my shoulder, and told me everything was going to be alright. Truly the nicest, most humble doctor I have ever met. If you ever read this sir, thank you. - Mark
author: @CoachClassic

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body: Mans really just did a open heart surgery and acted like he didn’t just save someone’s life... what a baller
author: @ZekeSpeakz

body: “As long as I don’t get nervous it’s fine” could you sound any more badass before cutting into someone’s heart?

title: if MIT were honest  #americancollege #mit #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague
body:
“weird and quirky, lol, random xD”
actual things at mit:
- clothing optional dorm floors with “exercise poles” installed
- explosives in the dorm courtyard
- annual “virgin sacrifice to the dark lord krotus” themed party
- wrestling in a pit of glitter lube or fake blood with a tesla coil blasting music
- annual piano drop, where a piano is dropped off of the roof
- real clubs include mixology, chocolate science, spinning balls of fire around your body as a performance art, and something called “assassins’ guild”
- decades old milk (the dorm pet)
- multiple dorms where students can paint literally whatever they want — from renditions of renoir to explicit materials to a fully black hallway with red lights
- before my time but a dorm floor once got in trouble for making a giant concrete replica of a bomb to promote a party called “drink ‘til you drop”
- also before my time, over half the lsd on the east coast was once made in an mit dorm
- also also before my time but us copyright law had to be rewritten because of an undergrad’s pet project pirating a ton of books and stuff

measuring bridges in smoots and putting police cars on the roof is honestly kinda tame for us tbh
author: @kimcoleeppling8177

title: if MIT were honest  #americancollege #mit #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague

title: if MIT were honest  #americancollege #mit #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague
body: I’ve always actually associated MIT as being more prestigious/academically rigorous than Harvard just on account that it is much more specialized and fine tuned towards STEM fields.
author: @Vastaya-shairei

title: if MIT were honest  #americancollege #mit #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague
body: That "going to school in Boston" thing is just a way to avoid saying we went to MIT because 1) it sometimes feels too braggy, esp. if we're around Asian parents, and 2) it avoids people having expectations of us being smart when we just want to chill
author: @jimmylin7233

title: if MIT were honest  #americancollege #mit #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague
body: I can tell you, from a European perspective, when we think of the pinnacle of engineering studies, MIT is the buzzword. That says a lot, MIT is a global brand, just as much as Harvard.
author: @blackdogue92

title: if MIT were honest  #americancollege #mit #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague
body: The MIT splash and spark programs really show the wierd and quirky sides of MIT, the campus is raided for a weekend/Sunday by high schoolers only to take classes on farts, Tolkien elvish and many other topics taught by MIT and other “Ivy league”/highly rated institutions..made the best memories and was the first taste of proper adult life as a kid
author: @LeahBouley

title: if MIT were honest  #americancollege #mit #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague
body: You know you’re a nerd when, if given the chance to go back in time and start over with a full scholarship to either Harvard or MIT, you’d pick MIT without any hesitation.
author: @augustinian2018

title: if MIT were honest  #americancollege #mit #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague
body: Funnily enough, if you go to Harvard or M.I.T., you do not go school in Boston.  Both schools are in Cambridge, Massachusetts.  They are on the same subway line.  Boston is across the Charles River from those two schools.  Boston has plenty of highly rated colleges and universities, however.
author: @rachelbonnar

title: if MIT were honest  #americancollege #mit #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague
body: In my experience MIT graduates are more likely to say they went to school in Cambridge, not Boston.  My favorite response to Harvard people saying they went to school in Boston is enthusiastically ask “oh, did you go to Emerson?”  If they say near Boston , it’s Tufts.  The squirming is delightful.
author: @johninitaly

title: If Berkeley were honest #berkeley #college #collegeadmissions
body:
Speak for yourself. UC Berkeley is freaking awesome! Berkeley Law is top tier with best IP and Environment & Energy law courses in the world. Life Sciences and Physics Departments are pioneers. Haas is one the best business schools. Engineering is the most sought after. So many more amazing faculty and students at one of the best universities in the world.

Also we call Cal. Go Bears!

title: If Berkeley were honest #berkeley #college #collegeadmissions
body: Most people would so much rather go to a school like Berkeley Duke or Hopkins than certain ivies like Cornell brown and Dartmouth. All those schools rank way higher
author: @celaine_

title: If Berkeley were honest #berkeley #college #collegeadmissions
body: Televised college football games include brief commercials from the competing schools extolling their virtues. A recent UCB video, after images of the lovely campus and bright eyed students, ended with the declaration that the university was "of the people, by the people, and for the people" of California. That reminded me that the school's spirit and purpose have always been to educate, stimulate and encourage young people who reflect the remarkable diversity of the state. That fundamental tenet separates UCB from otherwise excellent private schools. It is truly remarkable public university.
author: @18bhfbpt61

title: If Berkeley were honest #berkeley #college #collegeadmissions
body:
Berkeley student here. Stanford does not live rent free in my head, and I'm not a CS student! 😂

Berkeley has a great program for Rhetoric and Film & Media Studies. The former is rarely offered at the same depth anywhere else in the country. The latter, while significantly less focused on production than UCLA and USC to the south, is probably one of the best media theory educations you can get anywhere.
author: @MagicalMedic

title: If Berkeley were honest #berkeley #college #collegeadmissions
body: Bro is tripping. Bro most people call it Berkeley, Cal, UC Berkeley. Most people here love it because it’s a highly ranked and famous public school so tuition is much cheaper than private

title: If Berkeley were honest #berkeley #college #collegeadmissions
body: I just read somewhere last week that Stanford grads are unable to conduct an intelligent conversation. None of this surprises me. I dropped out of university at age 19 and returned ten years later to get my degree. The ignorance of that second batch of students was stunning. Most of them would not have made it through a course in high school English. The reason? They did not read anything they were not required to. They were functionally illiterate.
author: @merkin_muffley
